description: Global constraints applicable to all five zero-day IDS scenarios

malicious.definition: Network flows that represent actual attack activity matching scenario attack_cat from UNSW-NB15

benign.definition: Routine enterprise traffic (web browsing, DNS queries, SSH admin connections, file transfers) unrelated to attack progression

false_alarm.definition: Locally anomalous but globally common events; appear suspicious in isolation but represent benign activity

network_topology_reference.note: Network topology is now fully specified in terraform_network.json. See that file for infrastructure-level details.
network_topology_reference.observation_window_note: Used for temporal event sequencing in temporal_architecture_principles

unsw_grounding_principles.core_principle: Use UNSW-NB15 dataset as feature template library, NOT as pre-ordered sequences

key_insights.insight_1: UNSW-NB15 rows are independent flow observations with no inherent causal relationships
key_insights.insight_2: Extract bytes/packets/duration feature distributions from UNSW; synthetically sequence with realistic timings
key_insights.insight_3: Preserve UNSW value ranges; only modify timestamps and network labels during synthesis

workflow.step_1: Filter UNSW by scenario attack_cat and protocol/port constraints
workflow.step_2: Extract feature statistics (duration, bytes, packets, protocol, service)
workflow.step_3: Assess row count (determines TIER for synthesis)
workflow.step_4: Use filtered rows as templates for causal chain construction with synthetic timestamping

tiered_synthesis_framework.description: Event generation strategy depends on UNSW row availability after filtering

TIER_2.strategy: Use actual rows + parameterized feature-preserving variations
TIER_2.row_modification: Vary src/dst IPs (within subnet constraints), perturb duration ±20%, scale bytes ±15%
TIER_2.correlation_preservation: Ensure Corr(bytes, packets) and Corr(bytes, duration) remain consistent with template UNSW data

TIER_3.strategy: Use Gaussian KDE fitted to base rows; generate synthetic from distributions
TIER_3.row_modification: Generate feature values while preserving scenario-specific ranges and distributions
TIER_3.validation: Post-generate, re-validate against feature constraints; resample if needed

false_alarm_taxonomy.core_principle: Locally anomalous but globally common: suspicious in isolation, benign in practice

type_1.name: Unusual Port + Benign Service
type_1.description: High-numbered port from trusted admin host with common service
type_1.example: Port 45678 from Enterprise0 to DNS server (admin tool)
type_1.why_common: Admin tools use varied ports; DNS queries are routine

type_2.name: High Volume + Low-Risk Service
type_2.description: Large data transfer over normally low-volume protocol
type_2.why_common: DNS tunneling is a known benign technique

type_3.name: Rare Duration + Benign Context
type_3.description: Atypical connection duration from benign host
type_3.why_common: Failed SSH attempts common in enterprise

distribution_constraints.temporal_isolation: Must NOT be temporally adjacent to malicious attack chain
distribution_constraints.causal_independence: Must NOT form causal dependencies with malicious events

temporal_architecture_principles.description: 5-phase temporal structure simulating realistic attack progression

phase_1_benign_baseline.purpose: Establish normal baseline traffic before attack

phase_3_lateral_movement_exploitation.distribution: Sequential, cross-subnet transitions following topology rules
phase_3_lateral_movement_exploitation.purpose: Attack spreads within network, gains privilege

phase_5_benign_recovery.distribution: Randomly scattered; includes false alarm zones at 1200-1300s
phase_5_benign_recovery.purpose: Return to normal traffic patterns post-attack

temporal_architecture_principles.false_alarm_placement: Scattered in temporally isolated zones (NOT adjacent to malicious chain); typically 600-700s and 1200-1300s
temporal_architecture_principles.timestamp_ordering: All events strictly increasing; no temporal scrambling
temporal_architecture_principles.attack_window_coherence: All malicious events should cluster in ~900s window (not spread over entire 1800s)

output_schema.description: Output CSV structure for each scenario (30-event table); exact column order is CRITICAL for downstream NoDOZE alignment
output_schema.column_ordering: Exact order REQUIRED for all 23 columns

constraints.src_host_dst_host: Must map to defined hosts in network topology
constraints.src_subnet_dst_subnet: Inferred from hostname; must match topology
constraints.sttl: Source TTL (0-254); from UNSW direct pass-through
constraints.dttl: Destination TTL (0-254); from UNSW direct pass-through
constraints.state: Connection state (FIN, CON, INT, REQ, RST, URP, FOU, FSRA); from UNSW direct pass-through
constraints.sloss: Source packet loss count; from UNSW direct pass-through
constraints.dloss: Destination packet loss count; from UNSW direct pass-through
constraints.ct_src_dport_ltm: Count of source IPs to same dest port in last time interval; from UNSW direct pass-through
constraints.ct_dst_src_ltm: Count of unique dest IPs from same source in last time interval; from UNSW direct pass-through
constraints.attack_cat: UNSW categories: Normal | Exploits | Worms | Backdoor | Shellcode
constraints.label: Benign | Malicious | False Alarm (derived from label_distribution)

validation_checkpoints.description: Comprehensive sanity checks to validate each scenario's 30-event table

critical_checks.name: Host validity
critical_checks.description: All src_host and dst_host belong to defined topology
critical_checks.condition: All hosts in {User0-4, Enterprise0-2, Defender, OpHost0-2, OpServer0, external_*}

critical_checks.name: Subnet inference
critical_checks.description: src_subnet and dst_subnet correctly inferred from hostname
critical_checks.condition: map(hostname → subnet) matches topology mapping rules

critical_checks.name: Feature consistency
critical_checks.description: bytes, packets, duration are internally consistent and non-negative

critical_checks.name: TTL values
critical_checks.description: sttl and dttl in valid range (0-254 per RFC)

critical_checks.name: Attack category validity
critical_checks.description: attack_cat in {Normal, Exploits, Worms, Backdoor, Shellcode}
critical_checks.condition: All attack_cat values from UNSW categories

critical_checks.name: Attack coherence
critical_checks.description: All malicious events cluster in ~900s window (contiguous progression)

critical_checks.name: False alarm isolation
critical_checks.description: False alarms NOT temporally adjacent or causally linked to malicious chain
critical_checks.condition: False alarm events > 100s away from nearest malicious event

critical_checks.name: No null values
critical_checks.description: No NaN or null values in critical columns
critical_checks.condition: All critical columns (src_host, dst_host, etc.) have non-null values

critical_checks.name: Tracking columns present
critical_checks.description: _unsw_row_id and scenario_name columns are present and populated
critical_checks.condition: Both tracking columns exist and scenario_name matches expected value

unsw_dataset_reference.transformation_method: Pre-Step schema mapping (Gap 6: IP→host mapping, column alignment, feature aggregation)
unsw_dataset_reference.mapping_specification: See dataset_mapping.json for explicit column-level mapping (UNSW 45 columns → IDS 21 columns); includes identity mappings, aggregations, generated columns, inferred columns, and dropped features
unsw_dataset_reference.usage: Use TRANSFORMED dataset for all Steps 0-6; original UNSW is pre-processed in Pre-Step

transformation_details.maps_unsw_ips_to: Deterministic hostnames (User0-4, Enterprise0-2, Defender, OpHost0-2, OpServer0, external_XXX) using MD5(IP + scenario_id) hash
transformation_details.aggregates_features: sbytes + dbytes → bytes; spkts + dpkts → packets; infers service from dport and port-service lookup
transformation_details.adds_network_context: src_subnet, dst_subnet inferred from hostname mapping; generates sport (ephemeral 1024-65535)
transformation_details.preserves_feature_distributions: All UNSW values kept; only new columns added (no interpolation); Corr(bytes, packets) preserved ±5%
